prompt: Write an Adams CMD script to create a simple pendulum. It should have a single rigid link of length 200 mm and mass 1 kg, pinned to the ground at its top end by a revolute joint, with gravity acting in the -Y direction. Release it from 45 degrees.

assertions.text: Script creates a model with model create command
A rigid body part is created with part create rigid_body name_and_position
At least one marker is created on the link and at least one on ground
A revolute joint is created using constraint create joint revolute with i_marker_name and j_marker_name
Gravity is created with force create body gravitational (not force create body_force or any other variant)
Initial orientation set to 45 degrees for the release angle
All keywords spelled out fully (no abbreviations)
No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
Mass properties are set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties (not part create rigid_body mass_properties)
At least one geometry shape (sphere, cylinder, or box) is added to the moving link part
CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log (validated by scripts/run_adams_cmd.py)
